name: Borobia, Ciria and Malanquilla Loop
name_original: Río Manubles, Ciria y Molino de Malanquilla — circular desde Barobia
description: This moderate 37.6-mile road cycling route from Barobia explores the historic Manubles river valley, passing ancient towns and the Malanquil
region_intro: Road cycling around Borobia offers varied terrain at the foot of the Moncayo mountain range, providing diverse experiences for cyclists. The region features an irregular geography with high mountains and deep valleys, creating opportunities for both challenging climbs and moderate routes. The Manubles river valley traverses the municipality, adding scenic elements to local road cycling routes. Roads in the wider Soria province are generally well-paved and often quiet, ensuring an enjoyable cycling environment. Best road cycling routes around Borobia The most popular road cycling route is Aranda Comarca Loop, a 50.5 miles (81.2 km) trail that takes 3 hours 52 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through mountainous landscapes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Borobia, Ciria and Malanquilla Loop, a moderate 37.6 miles (60.6 km) path. This route explores the historic Manubles river valley, passing through ancient towns and offering diverse scenery. Local road cyclists also love the Cueva de Ágreda – Borobia loop from Malanquilla, a 58.8 miles (94.7 km) trail leading through varied terrain with significant climbs, often completed in about 4 hours 30 minutes. Road cycling around Borobia is defined by the Moncayo mountain range, the Manubles river valley, and quiet, well-maintained roads. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 90 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Borobia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling around Borobia?
A: Borobia, situated at the foot of the Moncayo mountain range, offers a diverse terrain for road cyclists. You'll find an irregular geography with both challenging climbs and more moderate routes through high mountains and deep valleys. The roads in the wider Soria province are generally well-paved and often quiet, providing an enjoyable cycling environment.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easier ride?
A: Yes, while the region is known for its varied terrain, there are moderate options available. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077398339/borobia-ciria-and-malanquilla-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Borobia, Ciria and Malanquilla Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 37.6-mile (60.6 km) route that explores the historic Manubles river valley, offering diverse scenery without extreme difficulty. Another moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31844171" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ciria – Borobia loop from Ciria&lt;/a&gt;, which is shorter at 35.5 miles (57.1 km).

Q: Where can I find challenging road cycling routes with significant elevation gain?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Borobia's proximity to the Moncayo mountain range provides excellent opportunities. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989952691/aranda-comarca-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aranda Comarca Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 50.5-mile (81.2 km) trail, feature significant elevation gain through mountainous landscapes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31844073" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de Ágreda – Borobia loop from Malanquilla&lt;/a&gt; is another difficult option, covering 58.8 miles (94.7 km) with substantial climbs.

Q: What natural features or scenic areas will I encounter on road cycling routes in Borobia?
A: Road cycling routes in Borobia often feature the dramatic backdrop of the Moncayo mountain range and traverse the scenic Manubles river valley. This valley is characterized by natural beauty, vineyards, and ancient towns. You'll experience diverse landscapes, from high mountains to deep valleys, offering picturesque surroundings.

Q: Are there any historical sites or cultural landmarks to see along the cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history. The Manubles river valley, for example, passes through ancient towns. The village of Borobia itself features historical sites such as the Parish Church of Nuestra Señora de la Asunción and the Hermitage of Nuestra Señora de la Asunción. These cultural landmarks can provide interesting stops during your ride.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Borobia?
A: The region's climate generally makes spring and autumn ideal for road cycling. These seasons offer pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery, avoiding the peak heat of summer and the colder conditions of winter. The clear skies are also a notable feature of the area.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available in Borobia?
A: Yes, many of the routes around Borobia are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077398339/borobia-ciria-and-malanquilla-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Borobia, Ciria and Malanquilla Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989952691/aranda-comarca-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aranda Comarca Loop&lt;/a&gt;, both offering a complete circular experience.

Q: What are some notable viewpoints or attractions near the road cycling routes?
A: The region offers several points of interest. You might consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3924334" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moncayo Summit&lt;/a&gt; for panoramic views, or exploring the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5271458" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valcongosto Gorge&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5288045" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de los Pilares&lt;/a&gt; is another natural highlight. The area is also known for its exceptionally clear night skies, home to the astronomical observatory 'El Castillo'.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Borobia?
A: There are several road cycling routes available around Borobia, with komoot featuring 7 distinct tours. These routes cater to various skill levels, ranging from moderate to difficult, ensuring options for most road cyclists.

Q: Is Borobia suitable for family-friendly road cycling?
A: While many routes feature significant elevation, some moderate loops might be suitable for families with older, more experienced children. The quiet, well-paved roads contribute to a safer environment. Always check the route's difficulty and elevation profile, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31844171" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ciria – Borobia loop from Ciria&lt;/a&gt;, to ensure it matches your family's capabilities.

Q: What is the typical duration for road cycling routes in Borobia?
A: The duration of routes varies depending on their length and difficulty. Moderate routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077398339/borobia-ciria-and-malanquilla-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Borobia, Ciria and Malanquilla Loop&lt;/a&gt; typically take around 2 hours 55 minutes. More challenging and longer routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31844073" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de Ágreda – Borobia loop from Malanquilla&lt;/a&gt;, can take approximately 4 hours 30 minutes.

Q: Are there any specific towns or villages that offer good starting points for road cycling in the area?
A: Borobia itself serves as an excellent starting point for many routes. Other nearby towns like Ciria and Malanquilla are also featured in route names, indicating they can be convenient access points for exploring the surrounding road cycling network.
tips: Borobia is a small, picturesque village that has found its appeal to tourists thanks to its clear skies, free of light pollution. This makes it ideal for stargazing.

Beautiful windmill that stands out on the plain of the municipality of Malanquilla. You can visit inside if we book a guided tour where they explain all the history and how it works.
At night, it is also beautiful since it is very well lit.
Reservations on the Malanquilla website

Located in the Manubles Valley we find another of the many charming towns with a lot of architecture and history in this area of Aragon. The imposing Castle stands out, of which the remains remain today, and the spectacular church of San Millán located on top of a rocky crag.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 60.5 km long and is estimated to take around 2 hours and 55 minutes to complete on a race bike. This duration is an estimate and can vary based on your cycling speed and stops along the way.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this route?
faqs.answer: This is categorized as a moderate difficulty race bike route. It involves an elevation gain of about 812 meters. While suitable for road bikes, some segments may be unpaved or challenging, requiring good fitness.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily follows roads suitable for race bikes. However, be aware that some segments might be unpaved or present challenges for a road bike. You'll cycle through varied terrain, including river valleys and old towns with potentially narrow, steep streets.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers diverse scenery and historical sites. You'll pass through the town of Borobia, and notable landmarks include the Malanquilla Windmill and the Castle and Church of San Millán de Berdejo. The Manubles river valley itself is known for its natural beauty, vineyards, and ancient towns.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The region experiences varied weather. To best enjoy the scenery and avoid extreme conditions, consider cycling during spring or autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be hot, and winter may bring challenging conditions.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Barobia, making it a circular route. You should be able to find parking within the town of Barobia to begin your ride.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required for this route?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are mentioned or typically required for cycling this route.

faqs.question: Is this route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As a race bike route, it is generally not suitable for dogs to accompany cyclists. If you are looking for dog-friendly activities, it's best to explore walking trails in the region instead.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route intersect with other paths. You will briefly encounter parts of the Vía Verde Santander-Mediterráneo (Valcorba - Ciria), Santander-Mediterráneo, and the SL-Z3 Sendero Local de la Cueva de la Cabrera. Berdejo.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through several towns, including Borobia and Ciria. These towns, and others in the Manubles Valley, may offer local cafes, restaurants, or accommodation options. It's advisable to check availability in advance, especially in this low-population density rural area.

faqs.question: Are there any specific challenges or tips for road cyclists on this route?
faqs.answer: While designed for road bikes, be prepared for potentially unpaved or challenging segments, particularly in older town areas with narrow, steep streets. Good fitness is recommended due to the moderate difficulty and elevation changes. The historical borderland nature of the region means you'll encounter a blend of landscapes and old fortifications.

waypoints.name: Castle and Church of San Millán de Berdejo
